# sync(coc): loom 370d560 — zero-tolerance.md rb-overlay retirement + Rule 1c

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `e38b918755a7` — sync(coc): loom 370d560 — zero-tolerance.md rb-overlay retirement + Rule 1c

**Body**:

Single-rule delta from loom commit 370d560 (feat(coc): zero-tolerance Rule
1c — "pre-existing" unprovable after context boundary).

The prior 60-line Ruby-specific zero-tolerance.md was a legacy whole-file
overlay (path-scoped to **/*.rb / **/Gemfile / **/*.gemspec) pre-dating the
slot-marker contract; compose.mjs returns exit 1 on that shape. Rather than
carry a dead overlay forward, this sync ships the loom global directly
(317 LOC, priority:0 / scope:baseline). Adds six sub-rules previously
missing from rb target: Rule 1a (scanner-surface symmetry), Rule 1b
(scanner deferral with tracking issue), Rule 1c (NEW — pre-existing
unprovable after context boundary), Rule 3a (typed delegate guards),
Rule 3c (documented kwargs accepted but unused), Rule 6a (public-API
removal requires deprecation cycle).

Scope shift: zero-tolerance.md baseline-emitted (always-on) instead of
Ruby-files-only. Matches py and rs targets — zero-tolerance is a baseline
concern across all languages.

Lost: Ruby-specific Why lines from the prior overlay. To be reintroduced
via slot markers when the slot-refactor lands as its own workstream.

VERSION: 1.10.0 → 1.10.1; upstream loom version stays 2.14.0; loom_sha
d4b4672 → 370d560.
